svg.text: Before / After
Why this is not just another "review" prompt with sharper wording.
Typical review mode
What usually happens
• starts from the current code only
• trusts polished current behavior too quickly
• blames the base model before falsifying wrapper layers
• produces generic advice like "improve prompt" or "clean memory"
• does not show the contamination path
• mixes evidence, theory, and style into one blob
What the user feels
Another plausible explanation. Still no proof. Still no hard fix order.
oh-my-agent-check
What changes
• starts from incidents, suspicion, and evidence windows
• forces a JSON artifact chain before conclusions
• maps symptom to mechanism, root cause, and source layer
• calls out stale evidence replay and hidden repair brains
• ranks failures by operational danger, not by pretty wording
• puts code-enforced fixes ahead of prompt cosmetics
What the user gets
A report that explains exactly where the wrapper betrayed the answer and what to harden first.
